notes: Day 19 (Mar 18) LATE 4:30PM ET. Sources: CNN live (3:57PM), AJ D19, ABC live, CNBC, NPR, Euronews, Wikipedia, Iran Intl.

ENERGY ESCALATION: Israel struck South Pars (world's largest gas field) + refineries with US coordination. FIRST attack on Iranian energy production. Oil $108. Bushehr nuclear plant hit (no damage per IAEA).
4TH KILL: Khatib (intelligence minister) confirmed dead by Pezeshkian. Katz authorized killing 'any senior Iranian official' without additional approval. Open-ended kill order.
IRAN RETALIATION: IRGC fired Khorramshahr-4 + Qadr MULTIPLE-WARHEAD missiles. Claims 100+ targets hit in Israel. 2 killed Ramat Gan. Cluster munitions over Tel Aviv.
CASUALTIES REVISED: HRANA: 1,354 civilian + 1,138 military + 622 unclass = ~3,114 Iran dead. Hengaw: 4,800+ military killed (D18). Lebanon: 968 killed. Iraq: 50+ killed.
ECONOMIC: Gas $3.84/gal (+31% from month ago). Vance: 'rough road ahead.' WH lifting US oil/gas shipment limits. Iraq resumed 250K bpd via Ceyhan (bypassing Gulf).

DIPLOMATIC: Riyadh emergency Arab/Muslim FM meeting. Turkey FM Fidan attending. Germany: 'would have advised against' war. Russia condemned Larijani killing. IAEA chief: can't eliminate nuclear program.

theoryBox: Day 19 evening — INFLECTION POINT: Israel struck South Pars, the world's largest natural gas field, with US coordination. This is the first attack on Iranian energy PRODUCTION facilities — crossing a threshold that was deliberately avoided for 19 days. Iran will almost certainly escalate further in response. With oil at $108 and climbing, the economic weapon is now being used BY BOTH SIDES against each other. Combined with 4 senior officials killed in 60 hours, Bushehr nuclear plant hit, and multiple-warhead missiles over Tel Aviv, the war is entering a phase where all constraints are dissolving simultaneously.
theoryBox_fa: روز ۱۹ عصر — نقطه عطف: اسرائیل پارس جنوبی (بزرگترین میدان گاز جهان) را با هماهنگی آمریکا زد. اولین حمله به تأسیسات تولید انرژی ایران. نفت ۱۰۸. بوشهر ضربه خورد. ۴ مقام در ۶۰ ساعت. موشک چندکلاهکی بر فراز تل‌آویو. همه محدودیت‌ها در حال فروپاشی.

metrics.desc: Gulf-wide barrage continues. Australian base in UAE nearly hit. Baghdad embassy attacked again.

sitrep: ENERGY WAR THRESHOLD CROSSED: Israel struck South Pars natural gas field — the WORLD'S LARGEST — and neighboring refineries, with US coordination (CNN/AJ/Wikipedia). First attack on Iranian energy PRODUCTION facilities in 19 days of war. This threshold was deliberately avoided until now. Iran vowed retaliation. Oil spiked to $108.

4TH SENIOR OFFICIAL IN 60 HOURS: Iran President Pezeshkian confirmed intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in overnight Israeli strike. Added to Larijani (Mon) + Soleimani (Mon) + Khatib (Tue). Katz issued open-ended authorization: killing 'any senior Iranian official' when opportunity arises, 'without additional approval needed.'

MULTIPLE-WARHEAD MISSILES: IRGC launched Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr multiple-warhead missiles at Israel in Larijani revenge. Claims hit 100+ military/security targets. 2 killed in Ramat Gan (near Tel Aviv). Cluster munitions captured on CNN cameras. Technology escalation — these are Iran's most advanced deployed systems.

BUSHEHR NUCLEAR PLANT HIT: Projectile struck premises of Iran's only operational nuclear power plant. IAEA: no damage or injuries. IAEA chief Grossi: war 'cannot entirely eliminate' Iran's nuclear program — 'material will still be there.' Nuclear dimension escalating.

Oil $108 + gas $3.84/gal: Brent spiked $5+ intraday on South Pars news (CNN). US gas +31% from month ago ($2.92→$3.84, AAA). Vance: 'rough road ahead for next few weeks.' White House temporarily lifting limits on domestic oil/gas shipments. Iraq resumed limited exports (250K bpd) via Turkey's Ceyhan port, bypassing Gulf.

CASUALTIES: HRANA updated: ~3,114 dead in Iran (1,354 civilian incl 207 children + 1,138 military + 622 unclass). Hengaw: 4,800+ military killed through D18. Lebanon: 968 killed (up from 886 in 2 days), 116 children. Iraq: 50+ killed. Israel: 17+ killed.

DIPLOMATIC: Riyadh hosting emergency Arab/Muslim foreign ministers meeting Wed. Turkey FM Fidan attending — only active diplomatic track. Germany's Merz: 'would have advised against' starting war, sees 'no convincing plan' for success. Russia 'firmly condemned' Larijani killing.

Australian PM Albanese: Iranian projectile struck near Australian military HQ in UAE — no injuries. Iran warned civilians to stay vigilant during Chaharshanbe Suri festivities, citing Israeli sabotage threats. Iran's internal security apparatus under extreme stress.
sitrep_fa: آستانه انرژی: پارس جنوبی زده شد. بزرگترین میدان گاز جهان. هماهنگی آمریکا. نفت ۱۰۸.
۴ مقام در ۶۰ ساعت: خطیب تأیید. کاتز: هر مقام ارشد بدون تأیید اضافی.
موشک چندکلاهکی: خرمشهر-۴ + قدر. ۱۰۰+ هدف اسرائیل. ۲ کشته رامات‌گان.
بوشهر ضربه خورد: آژانس: بدون آسیب. گروسی: جنگ نمی‌تواند برنامه هسته‌ای را حذف کند.
نفت ۱۰۸ + بنزین ۳.۸۴: +۳۱٪ ماهانه. ونس: جاده ناهموار. عراق ۲۵۰ هزار بشکه از جیحان.
تلفات: ۳,۱۱۴ ایران. ۴,۸۰۰+ نظامی (حقوق بشر). لبنان ۹۶۸. عراق ۵۰+. اسرائیل ۱۷+.
دیپلماسی: ریاض جلسه اضطراری. فیدان حاضر. آلمان: توصیه به عدم جنگ. روسیه: محکوم.
پرتابه نزدیک پایگاه استرالیا. هشدار چهارشنبه‌سوری.

dailySeries.sourceLabel: D1-D10: JPost. D11: UAE MoD. D12-D19: fragment est. D19: Iran retaliation barrage for Larijani — Qatar 13/14 ballistic intercepted + 2 killed Israel + Abu Dhabi kill + Baghdad embassy + Majnoon. Missile SPIKE from retaliation.

oil.note: Full daily series. CNBC/Investing.com confirmed where noted. D8 REVISED: Investing.com 52w high = $119.50, IEA confirmed 'within whisker of $120.' Previous estimate of $107 was intraday not peak. Markets closed regular hours Sun Mar 16. D16 = Sunday evening futures.

latestTargetBreakdown.note: Mar 18 (D19). Iran RETALIATION barrage for Larijani. Israel: 2 killed by ballistic + cluster munitions. Qatar: 13/14 ballistic intercepted. Abu Dhabi: 1 killed (shrapnel). Baghdad: embassy drones+rockets + Majnoon oil field. Saudi/UAE intercepting.

dailyRows.primary: All-theater: UAE (Al Dhafra, Dubai airport, Palm Jumeirah), Israel (Tel Aviv, Haifa), Saudi (Ras Tanura), Qatar (Al Udeid), Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan
dailyRows.capability: Operation Roaring Lion / True Promise IV launched within hours. 480 ballistic missiles + 720 drones on D1. Fastest Iranian response ever — suggests pre-positioned C2.
dailyRows.cost: Khamenei killed. IRIS Dena sunk by US submarine off Sri Lanka. Brent $72 (+$7). Hormuz shipping halted immediately — 150 ships stalled.
dailyRows.assessment: Overwhelming opening salvo. Iran distributing fire across 13 countries to overwhelm defenses.

dailyRows.primary: Peak launch day. UAE heaviest target. Israel: 124 injured from 10-warhead ballistic missile at Tel Aviv.
dailyRows.capability: 520 missiles + 850 drones — highest single-day volume. Iran expending stockpile rapidly.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $79.45 (CNBC). Iran state media confirms Khamenei family killed. Assembly of Experts emergency session. Jebel Ali Port fire, AWS data center hit.
dailyRows.assessment: Peak fury. Iran burning through inventory at unsustainable rate.

dailyRows.primary: Kuwait (6 US KIA at Shuaiba Port operations center), Bahrain, Saudi (US Embassy Riyadh drones), Israel (Hezbollah joins)
dailyRows.capability: 400+530. Kuwaiti F/A-18 friendly-fire shoots down 3 US F-15Es (all 6 crew survive). Hezbollah opens second front.
dailyRows.cost: 6 US KIA — first American combat deaths. Brent ~$83. Qatar arrests 10 IRGC cell operatives.
dailyRows.assessment: War expands. US casualties mount. Hezbollah + Iraqi militias = multi-front.

dailyRows.primary: Nuclear sites: Natanz entrance destroyed, Isfahan, Fordow access roads hit, Arak targeted
dailyRows.capability: 350+480. Declining from peak but still massive. Coalition strikes nuclear infrastructure.
dailyRows.cost: IAEA confirms Natanz inaccessible. Enrichment stockpile unverified 8+ months. Humanitarian crisis emerging.
dailyRows.assessment: Nuclear dimension activated. Escalation risk from enrichment uncertainty.

dailyRows.primary: Hezbollah long-range missile at Tel Aviv. Israel 250+ strikes on Lebanon. IRIS Dena confirmed sunk.
dailyRows.capability: 250+350. Clear decline. Senate rejects war powers resolution. CSIS: first 100 hours = $3.7B.
dailyRows.cost: Lebanon front fully active. Brent ~$90. Senate gives Trump political cover.
dailyRows.assessment: Decline accelerating. Political constraints loosening domestically.

dailyRows.primary: Qatar (14 ballistic + 4 drones per MoD), Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE
dailyRows.capability: 150+220. Fars claims >500 missiles, ~2,000 drones cumul. Rate ↓69% missiles, ↓74% drones.
dailyRows.cost: Brent ~$92. IEA triggers largest coordinated SPR release in history (400M bbl). War cost ~$2B/day.
dailyRows.assessment: Iran's ballistic capability declining structurally. Economic war intensifying.

dailyRows.primary: Continued Gulf + Israel harassment. Iraq: PMF HQs struck across multiple provinces.
dailyRows.capability: 100+160. Rate ↓79% missiles. >60% of Iranian launchers neutralized. 43 naval vessels destroyed.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $92.69 (CNBC). VLCC freight rates approaching records. Ruwais refinery shut (922K bbl/day offline).
dailyRows.assessment: Iran losing conventional capability. Asymmetric phase beginning.

dailyRows.primary: Diminished volume across Gulf. Iraq militia attacks intensify. F-35 shoots down Iranian Yak-130 — first F-35 air-to-air kill.
dailyRows.capability: 70+110. Rate ↓85%. Red Crescent: 65 schools, 32 medical facilities damaged.
dailyRows.cost: Brent intraday volatility. IEA Oil Market Report: 'largest supply disruption in history.' 20 Mb/d offline. Iran internet blackout persists.
dailyRows.assessment: Conventional campaign near-spent. Iran shifting to economic leverage.

dailyRows.primary: UAE MoD cumul: 1,422 UAVs detected (1,342 intercepted). All 8 cruise missiles intercepted.
dailyRows.capability: 50+80. Rate ↓90%. UAE air defenses showing strain but holding.
dailyRows.cost: Brent hits $119.50 — 52-week high (Investing.com). IEA: 'within whisker of $120.' Then crashes on SPR release news. Extreme volatility.
dailyRows.assessment: Oil price spike signals market panic. SPR intervention buys temporary relief.

dailyRows.primary: Mojtaba Khamenei designated new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts. NATO intercepts 2nd missile over Turkey.
dailyRows.capability: 40+60. Rate ↓92%. Trump claims war 'very complete, pretty much.' Then backtracks.
dailyRows.cost: Brent crashes to $88.43 (CNBC) — post-SPR selloff. EIA settles $94. Turkey deploys F-16s to N. Cyprus. 10,000+ civilian sites damaged in Iran.
dailyRows.assessment: Political transition in Iran. Conventional capability near-exhausted but regime intact.

dailyRows.primary: UAE reports continued intercepts. Gulf state coordination improving. Iraq: 300+ militia attacks cumul since D1.
dailyRows.capability: 27+81 (scaled from UAE MoD). Missile launches at lowest sustained level. Drones maintaining higher volume — cheaper, harder to suppress.
dailyRows.cost: Brent ~$91. Trump signals drawdown but Pentagon contradicts. Mixed messages on endgame.
dailyRows.assessment: Drone-missile divergence: missiles near-depleted but drone harassment continues at meaningful level.

dailyRows.primary: UAE MoD: 13 missiles (6 ballistic + 7 cruise) + 39 drones intercepted (official). Bahrain airport drones + fuel tanks fire. Ship hit in Hormuz.
dailyRows.capability: UAE cumul: 268 ballistic + 15 cruise + 1,514 UAVs. Fortune: Brent $90.96 AM. Drone attacks on airports emerging as pattern.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $91.98 close (CNBC). UKMTO: 16+ ships attacked cumul. Container ship fire in Hormuz.
dailyRows.assessment: Low-volume but high-disruption attacks on civilian infrastructure.

dailyRows.primary: Bahrain airport hit. Kuwait airport targeted. Dubai explosions. Abu Dhabi old airport fire. Container ship attacked near Jebel Ali.
dailyRows.capability: 8+40. KC-135 Stratotanker crashes western Iraq — 6 US KIA (total now 13). 4th US aircraft lost. Iran militias claim credit.
dailyRows.cost: Brent first >$100 close: $100.46 (CNBC). Fortune AM: $99.84. French CWO Frion killed near Erbil — first non-US allied death.
dailyRows.assessment: US losses mounting. Oil breaking $100 sustainably. French casualty internationalizes war.

dailyRows.primary: New Supreme Leader Khamenei first statement (written only — no video). Vows Hormuz stays closed. 3 more ships struck. Kuwait power lines hit by debris.
dailyRows.capability: 10+30. CENTCOM: 6,000+ US targets struck. 90+ Iranian vessels destroyed. Ballistic ↓86% from D1. UNSC Res 2817 condemns Iran 13-0-2.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $99.32 (CNBC). Khamenei statement fuels speculation on injuries. $10M bounty on Khamenei. State Dept: 'likely disfigured.'
dailyRows.assessment: Khamenei's absence and written-only statement suggests serious injuries. IRGC operating autonomously.

dailyRows.primary: UAE 9 ballistic + 33 drones intercepted (AJ). Qatar 4 ballistic + drones. Saudi 51 drones — LARGEST single-day barrage targeting kingdom. Kuwait airport drones. Jordan: 79/85 intercepted over week 2.
dailyRows.capability: 20+70. DRONE VOLUME TICKED UP — Saudi's 51-drone barrage a significant escalation. Kharg Island: 90+ military targets struck by CENTCOM (sparing oil). White House: ballistic ↓90%, drones ↓95%, 65+ vessels sunk.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $103.14 Fri close (CNBC). Fujairah oil hub fire from drone debris. IRGC declares UAE bases 'legitimate targets.' Trump 'not ready for deal.' May hit Kharg 'a few more times just for fun.'
dailyRows.assessment: Drone escalation despite missile depletion. Saudi barrage = Iran still has UAV stockpile. Kharg strike shows coalition restraint on oil — but for how long?

dailyRows.primary: Heaviest single-night bombardment since D1: Isfahan, Shiraz (20+ explosions), Tehran, Dezful, Khomein, Hamedan, Jask port. 6+ provinces. 10+ hypersonic missiles at Israel.
dailyRows.capability: 25+60. IRGC fires 10+ HYPERSONIC missiles at Israel — capability escalation. Israel critically low on ballistic interceptors (Semafor). IDF: 7,600+ strikes in Iran, 1,100+ in Lebanon cumul.
dailyRows.cost: 15+ killed Isfahan. Shiraz residential areas hit. 10,000+ homes destroyed (Tehran governor). 56 cultural sites damaged. Pope calls ceasefire. Pahlavi outlines transition. Houthis threaten Red Sea resumption.
dailyRows.assessment: Hypersonic escalation + interceptor crisis = war's most dangerous moment since D1. Houthi threat = potential second maritime chokepoint.

dailyRows.primary: Israel new Tehran strikes (smoke over capital). Dubai airport drone fire (flights suspended→limited). Abu Dhabi missile kill. Fujairah drone fire. Saudi 37 drones intercepted (AJ). Baghdad airport rockets. Israel: 245 attack waves total.
dailyRows.capability: DRONE VOLUME SURGED — Saudi 37 drones alone is highest single-day count from kingdom. IRGC claims most weapons cache intact and hasn't used post-2025 missiles yet. If true, Iran's offensive depth is much greater than ↓90% suggests. Israel planning 3+ more weeks of strikes.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $106+. Gulf exports ↓61% (Reuters/Kpler). US wounded revised to ~200. Hormuz coalition failing — Australia, Japan, Germany all declined. G5 'gravely concerned' re Lebanon. Trump threatens NATO umbrella + Xi summit.
dailyRows.assessment: IRGC 'arsenal intact' claim is the most significant D17 development. If true, Iran has been deliberately rationing — using old stock while preserving modern missiles. This reframes the entire attrition narrative. War may have much longer tail than assumed.

dailyRows.primary: DECAPITATION: Larijani + Soleimani confirmed killed. UAE full airspace closure. Qatar 13/14 ballistic intercepted. Saudi 12+ drones. Baghdad Al-Rasheed Hotel + embassy targeted. Abu Dhabi 1 killed. Tanker struck off Fujairah. Israel ground invasion of Lebanon.
dailyRows.capability: Decapitation accelerating but Qatar 13/14 ballistic missiles = Iran still has meaningful ballistic capability. IRGC 'cache intact' claim from D16 gaining credibility. Israel ground ops in Lebanon = second full front.
dailyRows.cost: Joe Kent (NCTC director) resigned — 'Iran not a threat.' Trump: don't need NATO. Delayed China trip 1 month. France: 'never' Hormuz during war. Amnesty: 170+ schoolgirls killed in US strike.
dailyRows.assessment: Crosscurrents: Military decapitation vs economic leverage. Israel destroying command structure but Iran's remaining capability + Hormuz control = strategic stalemate. First tanker transit could signal beginning of negotiated passage framework.

dailyRows.primary: SOUTH PARS STRUCK (world's largest gas field) + refineries, US coord. Khatib (intel minister) confirmed dead = 4th kill in 60h. Khorramshahr-4 + Qadr multi-warhead missiles at Israel, 2 killed. Bushehr nuclear plant hit. Gulf-wide barrage. Baghdad embassy again.
dailyRows.capability: ENERGY THRESHOLD CROSSED — first attack on Iranian energy production. Multi-warhead missile deployment = Iran's most advanced systems. IRGC claims 100+ targets in Israel. Katz: open kill order. But IAEA: can't eliminate nuclear program. War entering phase where all constraints dissolving.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $108 (+$5 intraday). Gas $3.84 (+31% monthly). Vance: 'rough road.' Germany: 'would have advised against.' Russia condemned. Riyadh emergency FM meeting. HRANA: 3,114 Iran dead. Hengaw: 4,800+ military. Lebanon 968. Iraq 50+.
dailyRows.assessment: INFLECTION POINT. South Pars + multi-warhead missiles + Bushehr + 4 kills = all previous constraints dissolving. War transitioning from managed campaign to potential total war. Economically unsustainable at current intensity for both sides.

timeline.title: Week 3 (Mar 16–22)
timeline.body: USS Tripoli arrives with 31st MEU. Possible initiation of Operation Epic Escort for Hormuz tanker convoys. Oil volatility continues — Brent likely $100-115 range. Houthi decision on Red Sea resumption imminent. Iran internet blackout entering 4th week.

timeline.title: Week 4–5 (Mar 23–Apr 5)
timeline.body: If Hormuz remains closed, SPR drawdowns accelerate. Oil may spike to $120+ if Houthis resume Red Sea attacks. Midterm election pressure builds on GOP — gas prices = top voter issue. First possible diplomatic probe window if backchannels exist.

timeline.title: Month 2 (Apr–May)
timeline.body: Sustained economic war phase. Iran's domestic situation deteriorates but regime shows no fracture signs. Lebanon ceasefire more likely than Iran ceasefire. Coalition strike tempo may slow as target sets exhausted.

timeline.title: Month 3+ (May onward)
timeline.body: If no deal, war becomes frozen conflict. Hormuz closure becomes new normal. Oil market adapts with permanent premium. OPEC+ non-Gulf producers benefit. Iran increasingly isolated economically but politically resilient.

timeline.title: Endgame variables
timeline.body: Khamenei's health/survival (if dead, succession crisis). Houthi Red Sea decision (second chokepoint = global crisis). China's role (only country with leverage over both sides). US midterm pressure (gas prices = GOP vulnerability).

timeline_fa.title: هفته ۳ (۱۶–۲۲ مارس)
timeline_fa.body: ناو تریپلی می‌رسد. شروع احتمالی عملیات اسکورت. نفت ۱۰۰–۱۱۵. تصمیم حوثی‌ها درباره دریای سرخ.

timeline_fa.title: هفته ۴–۵
timeline_fa.body: اگر هرمز بسته بماند، ذخایر سریع‌تر خالی می‌شود. نفت ممکن است ۱۲۰+ شود. فشار انتخاباتی.

timeline_fa.title: ماه ۲
timeline_fa.body: فاز جنگ اقتصادی. وضع داخلی ایران بدتر اما رژیم مقاوم.

timeline_fa.title: ماه ۳+
timeline_fa.body: جنگ منجمد. هرمز بسته = عادی جدید. نفت با حق‌بیمه دائمی.

timeline_fa.title: متغیرهای پایانی
timeline_fa.body: سلامت خامنه‌ای. تصمیم حوثی. نقش چین. فشار انتخاباتی آمریکا.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Escalation spiral / total war
scenarioProbabilities.body: NOW CO-LEADING. South Pars strike crossed the energy threshold. Iran will retaliate — likely against Saudi/Gulf energy infrastructure. Multiple-warhead missiles deployed. Katz: open kill order. Bushehr hit. All constraints dissolving. Each side escalating to force the other to back down. Classic escalation spiral with no off-ramp.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Protracted attrition
scenarioProbabilities.body: DECREASED from 42%. The South Pars strike makes 'protracted low-intensity' less likely — hitting energy production is an escalation that demands a response. War may be too intense to sustain at current level for months.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Declared victory + exit
scenarioProbabilities.body: Vance: 'rough road for next few weeks.' Admin still says 'weeks or sooner.' South Pars strike could be the culminating blow before declaration. But Iran's retaliation (multi-warhead missiles, 100+ targets) makes clean exit harder.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Regime fracture
scenarioProbabilities.body: 4 senior officials killed in 60h. Katz: open kill order on ALL senior officials. Hengaw: 4,800+ military dead. Internal security penetrated (Larijani intel from Tehran residents). If there's a breaking point, it's approaching. But Araghchi: 'structure doesn't depend on any single individual.'

scenarioProbabilities.name: Turkey-brokered ceasefire
scenarioProbabilities.body: INCREASED. Riyadh emergency FM meeting + Turkey FM Fidan = most diplomatic activity since war began. Germany publicly questioning war. Russia condemning. International pressure building. But both sides still refusing direct talks.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Negotiated deal
scenarioProbabilities.body: Low. Both sides rejected diplomacy D17-D19. But South Pars + $108 oil creates new urgency. Yuan passage talks still in background. Xi summit delayed but not cancelled.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: مارپیچ تشدید / جنگ تمام‌عیار
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: پارس جنوبی آستانه عبور. موشک چندکلاهکی. بوشهر. همه محدودیت‌ها فروپاشی.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: فرسایشی
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: کاهش. حمله انرژی = پاسخ لازم. شدت قابل ادامه نیست.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: اعلام پیروزی
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: ونس: هفته‌ها. پارس جنوبی = ضربه نهایی؟

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: فروپاشی رژیم
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: ۴ مقام ۶۰ ساعت. دستور کشتار باز. ۴,۸۰۰+ نظامی.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: آتش‌بس ترکیه
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: ریاض + فیدان. آلمان سؤال. روسیه محکوم.

theoryEvaluation.title: Energy war — ALL constraints dissolving
theoryEvaluation.body: South Pars strike is the single most consequential escalation since D1. For 19 days, coalition deliberately avoided Iranian energy production — Kharg Island raids spared oil infrastructure, Trump said 'just military targets.' Now Israel struck the world's largest gas field + refineries WITH US coordination. Bushehr nuclear plant also hit. Oil spiked to $108. Iran will almost certainly retaliate against Gulf energy infrastructure — Saudi Aramco, UAE ADNOC already under drone harassment. If Iran strikes Saudi oil production, Brent goes to $130+. The energy restraint that limited this war's economic damage is GONE.

theoryEvaluation.title: Military — technology escalation on both sides
theoryEvaluation.body: US: 5,000-lb bunker busters on Hormuz (Tue). Israel: South Pars strike (Wed). Iran: Khorramshahr-4 + Qadr MULTIPLE-WARHEAD missiles (first deployment in this war). Cluster munitions over Tel Aviv. These are the most advanced weapons each side possesses. Both sides are climbing the escalation ladder simultaneously. Katz issued open-ended kill authorization for ANY senior Iranian official. Hengaw: 4,800+ Iranian military dead. 4 senior officials in 60 hours. Iran's state is being decapitated while its remaining forces use their most advanced weapons.

theoryEvaluation.title: Political/diplomatic — pressure building but no mechanism
theoryEvaluation.body: Germany: 'would have advised against war, no convincing plan.' Russia: 'firmly condemned.' Riyadh emergency FM meeting = international alarm. Turkey FM only active mediator. Vance: 'rough road ahead.' Gas $3.84 (+31%). But Trump still refusing to negotiate. Khamenei: 'not time for peace.' Katz escalating. No off-ramp exists. The South Pars strike may force one — either Iran retaliates so hard it triggers full economic crisis, or the $108 oil + $3.84 gas forces Trump to seek exit. Either way, this is unsustainable at current intensity.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: جنگ انرژی — محدودیت‌ها فروپاشی
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: پارس جنوبی مهم‌ترین تشدید پس از D1. نفت ۱۰۸. ایران به زیرساخت انرژی خلیج حمله خواهد کرد.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: نظامی — تشدید فناوری
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: بمب ۵,۰۰۰ + پارس جنوبی + خرمشهر-۴ چندکلاهکی. پیشرفته‌ترین سلاح‌ها.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: سیاسی — فشار بدون مکانیزم
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: آلمان: توصیه ضد جنگ. روسیه: محکوم. ریاض: اضطراری. اما خروجی نیست. ناپایدار.

endstate.title: Most likely (35%)
endstate.body: Escalation spiral continues until economic pain forces a break. South Pars strike + $108 oil + $3.84 gas is politically unsustainable for both sides. Within 1-3 weeks, either: (a) Iran retaliates against Gulf energy, triggering global crisis that forces intervention; or (b) US domestic pressure ($4+ gas, midterms) forces Trump to accept a ceasefire he currently rejects. Turkey/Riyadh track is the most likely mechanism.

endstate.title: Second (30%)
endstate.body: Protracted but at LOWER intensity than current pace. Both sides exhaust advanced weapons, strikes slow, war becomes a frozen conflict around Hormuz/Lebanon with occasional flare-ups. Oil stabilizes $100-110. No formal end. This was the lead scenario before South Pars — it's still possible if both sides pull back from the energy threshold.

endstate.title: Wild card (8%)
endstate.body: Regime fracture. 4 officials killed in 60h. Open kill order. 4,800+ military dead. Intelligence penetrated from within. If Khamenei (injured) loses control of IRGC factions, Iran could fragment — either into collapse or into a more extreme hardline takeover that escalates to nuclear brinkmanship.

endstate_fa.title: محتمل‌ترین (۳۵٪)
endstate_fa.body: مارپیچ تا شکست اقتصادی. ۱–۳ هفته. ایران حمله به انرژی خلیج یا فشار بنزین آمریکا.

endstate_fa.title: دوم (۳۰٪)
endstate_fa.body: فرسایشی اما کم‌شدت‌تر. سلاح‌های پیشرفته تمام. هرمز/لبنان منجمد.

endstate_fa.title: غیرمنتظره (۸٪)
endstate_fa.body: فروپاشی. ۴ مقام ۶۰ ساعت. دستور باز. اگر خامنه‌ای کنترل از دست بدهد.

shippingRoutes.detail: UAE CLOSED ALL AIRSPACE March 17 as 'exceptional precautionary measure.' Dubai airport drone fire D17. Emirates only running limited flights. This route is not just dead — the entire UAE aviation system is periodically shutting down. IRGC declared UAE bases 'legitimate targets.'
shippingRoutes.eta: N/A — route permanently disrupted for duration of conflict

shippingRoutes.detail: Closed per on-ground intel despite official Turkish claims of neutrality. Turkey deployed F-16s and air defenses to Northern Cyprus. NATO intercepted 2 Iranian missiles over Turkish airspace (Gaziantep). Turkey–Iran land border effectively shut for commercial traffic. NATO missile over Gaziantep = Turkey is no longer a safe corridor.
shippingRoutes.eta: Not viable until conflict ends + diplomatic normalization

shippingRoutes.detail: Border confirmed open in every US Embassy Yerevan daily alert Mar 8-16. Normal flow: 400-450 trucks/day. Armenia maintaining neutrality. Key bottleneck: domestic Iran leg (Tehran→border, ~700km through active strike zones). HRANA confirms 3,065+ dead = entire country is combat zone. Internet blackout prevents coordination. IDF now striking Tehran, Tabriz (near Armenian border), Shiraz, Isfahan simultaneously.

Eastern borders not directly in combat zone but infrastructure is minimal. Pakistan border areas have own security challenges. Afghanistan route non-viable. Turkmenistan transit requires diplomatic arrangements that don't exist. No established book logistics on any of these routes.
shippingRoutes.eta: Months to establish if even possible

shippingRoutes_fa.detail: حریم هوایی امارات میدان جنگ. فرودگاه دبی متوقف. امارات هدف مشروع سپاه.
بسته علیرغم ادعای ترکیه. ناتو موشک بر فراز غازیان‌تپ رهگیری کرد.
مرز باز در گزارش‌های سفارت آمریکا. گلوگاه: مسیر داخلی ایران (۷۰۰ کیلومتر در مناطق بمباران).
زیرساخت ناکافی. امنیت ضعیف. مسیر کتاب وجود ندارد.

publishingStatus: Paper supply: CRITICAL. Iran imports virtually all pulp. Hormuz closed + rial collapsed → no imports. Existing warehouse stock only.

Internet blackout: 2+ weeks and WORSENING (CNN D17: 'trickle of remaining connectivity dropped abruptly'). Online book sales ↓80%+. No digital coordination for publishers, distributors, or authors. Third week of total communications isolation.
Power outages: Sustained across country from infrastructure damage (65 schools, 32 medical facilities hit per Red Crescent). Printing presses require stable power.
Isfahan: Second cultural capital and major publishing center. Struck heavily overnight Mar 14-15 (20+ explosions in Shiraz, Isfahan targeted). Cultural infrastructure at severe risk.
56+ cultural sites damaged. HRANA: 3,065 dead including in Isfahan, Shiraz (major cultural/publishing centers both under sustained bombardment). Tabriz now also being struck.
New title production: Effectively halted. Only pre-war warehouse stock available. Distribution networks disrupted by strikes across 30 of 31 provinces.

Assessment: Iranian publishing industry in existential crisis. Even post-war, recovery will require months for paper imports, years for full reconstruction. Pre-war book stocks are finite and depleting — priority procurement window is NOW for any accessible inventory.
publishingStatus_fa: کاغذ: بحرانی. واردات متوقف. فقط موجودی انبار.
قطع اینترنت: ۲+ هفته و بدتر شده. فروش آنلاین ↓۸۰٪+. هفته سوم انزوای ارتباطی.
قطع برق: گسترده. چاپخانه‌ها نیاز به برق پایدار.
اصفهان: بمباران سنگین ۱۴-۱۵ مارس. زیرساخت فرهنگی در خطر.
۵۶+ سایت فرهنگی آسیب‌دیده. واقعی احتمالاً بیشتر.
تولید عنوان جدید: متوقف. فقط موجودی قبل جنگ.
ارزیابی: بحران وجودی نشر ایران. بازسازی ماه‌ها تا سال‌ها.
impactIranfarhang: PRIMARY ROUTE DEAD: Tehran→Dubai→Atlanta via Emirates is completely non-functional. Dubai airport suspended flights Mar 16 from drone fire. 3rd airport drone incident in 4 days. This route will not reopen during conflict.

BEST ALTERNATIVE: Armenia (Agarak/Norduz) border confirmed open but domestic Iran leg (700km) through active combat zones is extremely hazardous. Coalition struck 6+ provinces overnight Mar 14-15. No internet for coordination.

SUPPLY SOURCE: Publishers in Iran cannot produce new titles — paper imports halted, power outages, internet blackout entering 3rd week. Only pre-war warehouse stock available. Isfahan (major publishing center) under bombardment.

OFAC STATUS: Informational materials exemption (31 CFR §560.210(c)) remains valid — statutory protection via Berman Amendment/IEEPA. Not affected by war. Payment via KEMCO SARL (France) third-country structure still viable in principle but practical execution depends on shipping route.
FINANCIAL: Rial in freefall. Any procurement denominated in rial is cheaper but requires functioning transfer mechanism. KEMCO route may face increased scrutiny from French compliance.

WINDOW: Pre-war book stocks are finite and depleting. If Armenia route can be established, priority procurement of available inventory should begin immediately. Every week of delay reduces available stock.
impactIranfarhang_fa: مسیر اصلی مرده: تهران←دبی←آتلانتا کاملاً متوقف.
بهترین جایگزین: ارمنستان — مرز باز اما مسیر داخلی ایران پرخطر.
منبع تأمین: ناشران نمی‌توانند عنوان جدید تولید کنند. فقط موجودی انبار.
وضعیت OFAC: معافیت مواد اطلاعاتی همچنان معتبر.
مالی: ریال سقوط آزاد. پرداخت از طریق KEMCO ممکن اما عملی مشکل.
فرصت: موجودی قبل جنگ محدود. تدارک فوری از طریق ارمنستان.
prepIranfarhang: Activate Armenia route exploration: Contact Yerevan-based logistics contacts. Assess Agarak/Norduz border crossing capacity for book freight.
Identify Tehran warehouse partners willing/able to load and dispatch to Armenian border despite domestic conditions (no internet, active strikes).
Pre-negotiate Yerevan→port (Poti, Georgia or Batumi)→sea freight→US routing and costs.
Accelerate procurement of any accessible pre-war inventory — prioritize high-demand university titles.
Brief university library clients on supply disruption timeline and potential delays. Set expectations for 3-6 month procurement window.
Monitor KEMCO payment channel capacity — confirm French bank compliance posture hasn't changed since war began.
Prepare contingency for total supply interruption lasting 6+ months.
prepIranfarhang_fa: بررسی مسیر ارمنستان: تماس با شرکای لجستیکی ایروان.
شناسایی انبارهای تهران آماده ارسال به مرز ارمنستان.
اطلاع‌رسانی به کتابخانه‌های دانشگاهی درباره تأخیرات.
signalsIranfarhang: WATCH: Houthi decision on Red Sea attacks — if resumed, alternative sea routes from Georgia/Turkey also disrupted. This is the highest-impact near-term variable for ALL shipping routes.
WATCH: Armenia border status in US Embassy Yerevan daily alerts (has been open every day Mar 8-16).
WATCH: Any resumption of limited internet in Iran — would indicate regime stabilization and enable supplier coordination.
WATCH: Dubai airport operations — currently suspended. If flights resume = UAE risk slightly lower. If attacks continue = route dead indefinitely.
WATCH: Rial exchange rate — further collapse makes procurement cheaper but transfer harder.
WATCH: IEA/OPEC monthly reports for Hormuz shipping resumption signals.
signalsIranfarhang_fa: نظارت: تصمیم حوثی درباره دریای سرخ — اگر از سر گرفته شود، مسیرهای جایگزین هم مختل.
نظارت: وضعیت مرز ارمنستان در گزارش سفارت آمریکا.
نظارت: بازگشت اینترنت محدود در ایران.
impactKIP: Iran-UAE corridor: Near-total disruption. Dubai airport suspended Mar 16. IRGC declared UAE bases 'legitimate targets' Mar 14. UAE-Iran banking risk EXTREME — UAE+Saudi leaders jointly condemned 'sinful Iranian attacks.'
UAE asset risk: MEDIUM-HIGH and rising. UAE considering freezing Iran-linked assets (TRT World/Chatham House). IRGC target declaration makes physical assets in UAE targets for diplomatic retaliation.
Hormuz: Closed. ~15 Mb/d removed from global supply. No escort operations despite Trump's plea. USS Tripoli deploying but no timeline for tanker convoys. All import-export via Hormuz is halted.
Dubai logistics hub: Severely degraded. Airport operations suspended. Port operations intermittent. Insurance premiums prohibitive for Iranian-linked cargo.
Banking: UAE banks likely tightening KYC/AML on Iran-connected accounts preemptively. French (KEMCO-adjacent) and UAE banking channels both under increased compliance pressure.
Physical security: UAE intercepted >1,800 Iranian projectiles. Civilian casualties including Abu Dhabi missile kill Mar 16. Operating environment for Iranian business personnel in UAE is dangerous.
impactKIP_fa: مسیر ایران-امارات: تقریباً مختل. فرودگاه دبی متوقف. سپاه پایگاه‌های امارات را هدف مشروع اعلام کرد.
دارایی‌ها در امارات: خطر بالا و در حال افزایش.
هرمز: بسته. ~۱۵ م.ب/ر حذف. اسکورت نیست.
هاب لجستیکی دبی: به شدت تضعیف شده.
prepKIP: Assess all KIP assets in UAE for exposure to both military risk and potential asset freeze.
Establish emergency communication protocols that don't depend on Iranian internet (satellite phone, third-country relay).
Diversify banking away from UAE — consider Oman, Turkey, or other jurisdictions less directly involved in conflict.
Suspend any new UAE-bound shipments until Hormuz reopens and airport operations normalize.
Prepare legal contingency for potential UAE asset freeze or enhanced sanctions screening.
Identify alternative logistics hubs: Oman (Muscat/Sohar), Turkey (Mersin), Georgia (Poti/Batumi).
Monitor insurance market — current premiums for Gulf operations may be prohibitive.
Brief all personnel in UAE on physical security — avoid proximity to military installations, airports, and oil infrastructure.
prepKIP_fa: پروتکل ارتباطی اضطراری بدون اینترنت ایران.
تنوع‌بخشی بانکی به عمان، ترکیه یا گرجستان.
توقف محموله‌های جدید تا بازگشایی هرمز.
آمادگی حقوقی برای انجماد احتمالی دارایی.
شناسایی هاب‌های جایگزین: مسقط، مرسین، پوتی.
signalsKIP: CRITICAL: UAE asset freeze announcements — any move to freeze Iran-linked assets would require immediate action.
CRITICAL: Houthi Red Sea decision — if resumed, Suez alternative route also disrupted, isolating Gulf further.
WATCH: UAE-Iran diplomatic communications — any de-escalation signals reduce asset risk.
WATCH: Hormuz escort operations — USS Tripoli arrival + French 10-ship proposal = possible reopening timeline.
WATCH: UAE banking sector circulars on Iran-related compliance.
WATCH: Dubai airport operations resumption — indicator of normalization pace.
WATCH: Insurance market premiums for Gulf shipping and operations.
WATCH: China-Iran trade flows — if Chinese vessels transit Hormuz successfully, may signal partial reopening path.
signalsKIP_fa: بحرانی: تصمیم حوثی درباره دریای سرخ.
